title: IMO approves net-zero regulations for global shipping; adoption session subsequently adjourned to October 2026
authors_or_org: International Maritime Organization (IMO)
claim_supported: IMO's MEPC 83 (7-11 April 2025) approved the Net-Zero Framework: mandatory well-to-wake GHG fuel-intensity limits plus a global pricing/reward mechanism for ships over 5,000 GT (85% of international shipping CO2). title: CARB to repeal Advanced Clean Fleets' High Priority Fleet and Drayage Fleet zero-emission provisions
authors_or_org: California Renewable Transportation Alliance (ca-rta.org), reporting on California Air Resources Board (CARB) action
claim_supported: CARB's Advanced Clean Fleets regulation required newly registering drayage trucks at California seaports/intermodal railyards to be zero-emission from 1 Jan 2024, with a full zero-emission drayage fleet target by 2035. Following a legal settlement with a 17-state coalition (led by Nebraska) and trucking-industry groups, CARB voted (25 Sept 2025) to repeal the High-Priority Fleet and Drayage Fleet provisions, with repeal expected to take effect 1 January 2027 (formal action required by 31 August 2026); CARB separately withdrew its EPA waiver request for the ACF's 2036 100%-ZEV-sales mandate in January 2025 and cannot enforce that mandate without a new waiver -- a live, confirmed regulatory reversal directly affecting port/drayage equipment electrification demand.
locator: California Renewable Transportation Alliance (ca-rta.org) article, fetched directly this session.
notes: RECLASSIFIED: the primary regulator page (ww2.arb.ca.gov) returned HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed on every fetch attempt across both the scout and this verifier session (2 separate attempts, same and different CARB URLs) -- fetch routes on the official CARB domain are exhausted. Source_type changed from 'regulator' to 'market_industry' and tier confirmed T2 to honestly reflect that the CARB page itself was never independently fetched, only trade-association reporting on the CARB action.

notes: Official CharIN consortium page fetched successfully by scout; formally published IEC technical specification, not vendor marketing. Not independently re-verified this session; carried forward unchanged.

title: Hitachi Construction Machinery / ABB full-battery mining dump truck (EH4000) reaches commissioning at Kansanshi mine, Zambia
authors_or_org: Hitachi Construction Machinery; ABB; First Quantum Minerals (via International Mining trade-press reporting)
claim_supported: ABB and Hitachi Construction Machinery jointly developed (since June 2021) the Hitachi EH4000 'Full Battery Dump Truck,' the world's first ultra-large fully electric haul truck, using a hybrid battery + trolley-assist (overhead power line) + regenerative-braking system. A technological feasibility trial ran at First Quantum Minerals' Kansanshi copper-gold mine (Zambia) from June 2024 to August 2025, during which the truck covered over 4,000 km and transported more than 30,000 tonnes; a follow-on 'Battery Truck & Electrification Showcase' technical demonstration for mining customers was held at Kansanshi on 15-16 April 2026, with commercial deployment targeted for fiscal year 2027.
locator: International Mining (im-mining.com) article, 16 April 2026, fetched directly this session.
notes: RECLASSIFIED and UPDATED: the primary ABB press release (new.abb.com) failed to fetch (timeout, then socket error) across two attempts in two sessions; a direct Hitachi Construction Machinery press-release page (hitachicm.com) also returned HTTP 403 this session. Instead, International Mining trade-press reporting was fetched successfully this session, providing more current and more precise detail (EH4000 model name, exact trial distance/tonnage, FY2027 commercial-deployment target) than the raw record's ABB-sourced claim. Source_type changed from 'market_industry' to 'trade_press' to honestly reflect the actually-fetched source.

title: Singapore Introduces New Standard for Electric Harbour Craft (e-HC) Charging Infrastructure (Technical Reference TR 136)
authors_or_org: Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore (MPA); Enterprise Singapore
claim_supported: MPA and Enterprise Singapore published Technical Reference (TR) 136 (25 March 2025) establishing safety requirements for charging infrastructure and battery-swap systems for electric harbour craft (e-HC), protecting personnel/property from electrical and mechanical hazards and promoting interoperability (aligned with CCS, IEC 61851, IEC 62840); from 2030 all new harbour craft operating in the Port of Singapore must be fully electric, B100-biofuel-capable, or compatible with net-zero fuels.
locator: MPA media centre release, 25 March 2025 (Technical Reference 136)
notes: Official MPA (Singapore government statutory board) page fetched successfully by scout. Not independently re-verified this session; carried forward unchanged.
